title: Teaching
text: I teach risk management and insurance courses across the undergraduate, master’s, and Ph.D. programs at FSU. For current schedules, see the FSU Department of Risk Management/Insurance.

title: Teaching
text:
Florida State University

RMI 3011 — Risk Management/Insurance Undergraduate. Introduction to the principles of risk management and insurance and their application to personal and business pure risk problems. [Catalog]


RMI 4115 — Life/Health Insurance Undergraduate. Personal and business life and health insurance needs, plan characteristics, rating, underwriting, and financial analysis. [Catalog]


RMI 4292 — Property and Casualty Insurer Operations Undergraduate. The composition, financial structure, and operations of the property-casualty insurance industry, with special consideration of consumer problems and regulatory responses. [Catalog]


RMI 5710 — Insurance Company Operations Master’s (online). Fundamentals of risk, the management of pure risk, insurance mechanisms, insurer operations, and the evolution of enterprise risk management. [Catalog]


RMI 6395 — Seminar on the Theory of Risk and Insurance Ph.D. Review of the theoretical foundations of risk and insurance: the concept of risk, contributions from related disciplines, determinants of insurance consumption and risk management decisions, and industry dynamics. [Catalog]

title: Teaching
text:
University of Georgia

RMIN 4000 — Risk Management and Insurance Undergraduate (Summer 2014 – Spring 2017). Risk identification, risk evaluation, and risk treatment methods, with emphasis on insurance as a risk management tool. Designed for non-majors as well as a foundation for advanced RMI coursework.

title: Teaching
text:

Yuchen Qi (Finance, University of North Texas).
Dan Yang (Risk Management and Insurance, University of Georgia, 2022) — Troy University.

title: Research
text: My research focuses on empirical insurance economics, with emphasis on financial accounting, taxation, regulation, and enterprise risk management. For a full record, see my CV.

title: Evan M. Eastman
text:
I am the Independent Life and Accident Insurance Associate Professor and Research Coordinator for the Risk Management and Insurance Center at Florida State University in the Herbert Wertheim College of Business. I serve as a faculty member in the Department of Risk Management/Insurance, Real Estate, and Legal Studies in the Dr. William T. Hold/The Alliance’s Program in Risk Management and Insurance.
My research focuses on empirical archival insurance economics. Active areas include financial accounting, taxation, corporate risk management, and real estate.

title: Evan M. Eastman
text:
Education

Ph.D. Risk Management and Insurance, University of Georgia, 2017
B.S. Economics, The Pennsylvania State University, 2012

April 2024 · Eastern Finance Association Annual Meeting; St. Petersburg, Florida — Bank Risk Management and Systemic Risk: Global Evidence (Samuel W. Adams, Larry Fauver, Alvaro G. Taboada, and Lauren Milbach).
August 2023 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Washington, D.C. — How Life Insurer Attributes Affect Consumer Purchase Behavior: Experimental Insights on the Role of Financial Strength, Sustainability, Service Quality and Type of Customer Touchpoint (Moritz Hanika and Nadine Gatzert).
August 2021 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Virtual — Contemporaneous versus Traditional Measures of Reserve Errors: The Effect of Monitors on Earnings Management (Martin F. Grace and J. Tyler Leverty).
June 2021 · Canadian Academic Accounting Association Annual Meeting; Virtual — Taxes and Corporate Investment Behaviour: Evidence from the Halloween Massacre of 2006 (Matthew Boland and Khin Phyo Hlaing).
November 2020 · Southern Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Virtual — Enterprise Risk Management and Mergers and Acquisitions: Evidence from the Insurance Industry (Yunhe Li, Steve Miller, and Tina Yang).
August 2020 · World Risk and Insurance Economics Congress; Virtual — Tort Reform and Physician Moral Hazard (Juan Zhang).
August 2019 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; San Francisco, California — Conditional Conservatism and Insolvency Risk (J. David Cummins, Mary A. Weiss, and Juan Zhang).
August 2018 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Chicago, Illinois — Improving Performance of Non-Insurance Companies with Insurance Companies (Jing Rong Goh) and Underwriting Discipline and Investment Risk Taking in the Property-Liability Insurance Industry (Anastasia Ivantsova and J. Tyler Leverty).
August 2017 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Toronto, Ontario — Modeling the Risk of a Policyholder Run (Tobias Huber and Johannes G. Jaspersen).
August 2016 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Cambridge, Massachusetts — Are Loss Reserves and Internal Capital Markets Substitutes? (Thomas R. Berry-Stölzle, James M. Carson, and In Jung (Anna) Song).
August 2015 · World Risk and Insurance Economics Congress; Munich, Germany — Enterprise Risk Management Sophistication and Firm Risk (James Barrese, Stephen G. Fier, David M. Pooser, and Paul L. Walker).
August 2014 · American Risk and Insurance Association Annual Meeting; Seattle, Washington — CEO Overconfidence, Litigation Risk, and the Demand for Directors and Officers Insurance (Jeffrey Boles, Yevgeniy Davydov, and Jacqueline Volkman-Wise).
